body_html:
Page 1

Bearing 315                          Supplemental Schedule                          August 22, 1996

A number of changes to the Event Schedule were made after the last issue went to press so we have sent out a supplement.

* Please note that the proposed COC September Trollhaugen event has been cancelled.

* A new event by COC that was not on the original schedule will be held on the Ros-Elum map on Saturday September 21. This will complement the EOC score O event at Cle-Elum Ridge the next day (Sunday). Camping will be available for $5.00 at Camp Koinonia and a barbeque will be held at Kent Verbeck's house in Cle Elum on Saturday night.

Starts for Saturday's event will be from 11 am to 1 pm. Courses will be by Don Denbo.
Hope to see you there!                  Don Atkinson        Meet Director      (206)488 0270

* The BC Championships, originally listed for Sept. 28/29, are now on October 5th (individual A event) and 6th (3 person relay). The fees are C$15 plus C$10 for the relay, $5 off each for juniors, and $5 /day late fee if you don't enter before the Sep 21 deadline. This is on a new map just beyond Brandywine Falls Campsite on Highway 99 about 10 Km. S of Whistler.

* And finally, the Fort Casey event originally scheduled for October 5th, is now rescheduled for Sunday October 13th, with starts 11 till 1 pm.

Results: Australian visitors, Ian Kluckow and Rip Heminway won the Old Man Pass Six hour Rogaine held about 30 miles Northeast of Vancouver WA on Saturday August 17. Having tied for the most points (1370 out of 1540 possible) with the second place team of Jan Urban and Jana Urbanova, Ian and Rip had the lowest elapsed time. The meet was held under near perfect conditions, dry and partly cloudy. 21 teams with a total of 43 rogainers took part. Thanks to the organisers, Les Stark, Terradan Landchild and John Britton and all others who helped.
